summary: This is a podcast interview between Justin Brennan (multifamily mentor) and Kristen Williams (event strategist) focused on Kristen's entrepreneurial journey, event production expertise, and coaching business models. While Justin mentions his multifamily schooling community vision, the conversation centers on Kristen's background in event strategy for Tony Robbins, Dean Graziosi, and Russell Brunson rather than deal-specific multifamily instruction.
takeaways: Event conversion framework: Structure content in specific order with offer at specific time to achieve 20% conversion on high-ticket offers ($110k+)
Virtual event show-up rate: 85% attendance for paid events with strong email sequences and swag
Three-day event structure: Deliver content themed around core promise (e.g., 'Get Your First Deal'), then make offer on day 2-3 with repitch strategy in subsequent sessions
Marketing timeline: Begin advertising 8 weeks before event; pull from existing email list and supplement with paid ads and free masterclass funnels
Tiered offer strategy: Create price anchors (e.g., $10k coaching vs $25k done-for-you) to capture both new and advanced buyers without confusion
Event logistics formula: Start with sales strategy and offer design first, then reverse-engineer content to taste the offer, then handle production/logistics/team training
Subscriber model risk: Subscriptions create high churn; structure as 12-month commitment with juicy deliverables at end to prevent cancellation

skill_candidates.evidence: Conversation does not address deal analysis, underwriting metrics, or deal evaluation frameworks relevant to Brian's stated drag in deal sourcing and underwriting
No content about broker relationships, deal sourcing networks, or broker outreach strategies for multifamily properties
No geographic market analysis, market selection criteria, or market research methodology discussed

skill_candidates.change: Apply tiered event model to filter and segment coaching students by deal progress stage
skill_candidates.evidence: Kristen demonstrates 'graduation model' with launch levels and impact levels; Justin can create tiers where students advance from Level 1 (first 10-20 units) to partnership/mastermind tiers, creating clear progression goals and deal collaboration pathways that align with his 10,000-unit vision

title: From Struggles To Success! Inspiring Entrepreneurial Journey For Women In Business: Kristen Williams
